channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Reale entra en el capital de Canitas con un 18,9% para impulsar los servicios para mascotas
item.description: Reale Group ha alcanzado un acuerdo con Canitas, compañía especializada en servicios veterinarios por suscripción, que incluye la entrada de la aseguradora en su accionariado con una participación del 18,9%. La alianza se enmarca en la estrategia de diversificación de Reale y en el desarrollo de un ecosistema de servicios orientado al ámbito de las mascotas. Canitas , por su parte, materializa un acuerdo estratégico y comercial que contribuirá a impulsar su crecimiento y sus planes de desarrollo para los próximos años y mantiene a Aurica Capital -con el 60% del capital desde 2023- como socio mayoritario y continuará con la gestión de la compañía.

item.title: Sabseg suma y sigue: ahora integra a la correduría Baigorri en Aragón
item.description: Sabseg sigue con paso firme en su estrategia de crecimiento y consolidación en la Península Ibérica y, según puede adelantar de nuevo esta Redacción, hoy hará pública la incorporación de la correduría de seguros Baigorri , especializada en riesgos empresariales e industriales , con sede en Zaragoza . Fundada y dirigida por Eduardo y Raquel Baigorri , la correduría aragonesa cuenta con una trayectoria de más de 30 años en el mercado. No es el primer paso de Sabseg en Aragón pues a finales de 2024 ya integró PG Corredores, con sede en Zaragoza y especializada en grandes riesgos industriales, técnicos y financieros.

item.title: Ferrer&Ojeda abre oficina en Palma de Mallorca
item.description: Ferrer&Ojeda continúa avanzando en su estrategia de crecimiento territorial con la apertura de una nueva oficina en Palma de Mallorca, fruto de una alianza con Grupo Autovidal, empresa familiar centenaria vinculada al sector industrial y de la automoción y que cuenta con correduría de seguros propia . Según el presidente de Ferrer&Ojeda, Luis Ferrer, «la alianza permite sumar fuerzas con una familia empresaria con la que se comparten valores, combinando la especialización con el conocimiento del mercado local para mejorar la proximidad con los clientes». Por parte del Grupo Autovidal, sus responsables señalan que la integración en Ferrer&Ojeda permite ampliar capacidades y acceso a soluciones aseguradoras, manteniendo el arraigo al territorio y los valores de proximidad y confianza.

item.title: Wildfires Used to ‘Sleep’ at Night. Climate Change Has Them Burning Overtime
item.description: Burning time for North American wildfires is going into overtime. Flames are lasting later into the night and starting earlier in the morning because human-caused climate change is extending the hotter and drier conditions that feed fires, a new study found. Fires used to die down or even die out at night as temperatures dropped and humidity increased, but that’s happening less often.

item.title: Xceedance Names Allapur Global Chief People Officer
item.description: Xceedance named Swapna Allapur chief people officer. She joins Xceedance from Everest Group, where she was chief people officer for four years. Swapna will lead the company’s global people strategy, focusing on building workforce readiness, enabling large-scale transformation and strengthening the organizational capabilities.

item.title: Record Drought Sparks Worries About Fires, Water Supply And Food Prices
item.description: Meteorologists said it’s a bad sign for the upcoming wildfire season, food prices and western water issues. Drought in the contiguous United States has reached record levels for this time of year, weather data shows. Because of record heat, much of the West has had exceptionally low levels of snow in the first few months of the year, which is usually how the region stores water for the summer.

item.title: Travelers Profit Rises on Stronger Underwriting, Lower Catastrophe Losses
item.description: Property and casualty insurance giant Travelers reported a jump in first-quarter profit, buoyed by robust underwriting gains and lower catastrophe losses than a year earlier when results were severely hit by the Los Angeles wildfires. Travelers reported a core profit of $1.7 billion, or $7.71 per share, compared with $443 million, or $1.91 per share, a year earlier. “Catastrophe losses for the quarter totaled $761 million pre-tax, with the largest events being the winter storm that impacted much of the country in January and a large tornado-hail event in March,” said CFO Dan Frey on a call with analysts.

item.title: Aon New Zealand elevates Michael Twyman to specialty GM
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: APAC Q1 nat cat losses moderate despite high humanitarian impact: Gallagher Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AXA XL appoints Jiten Halai as Global CUO, Structured Risk Solutions
item.description: AXA XL, the property and casualty and specialty risk division of AXA, has appointed Jiten Halai as Global Chief Underwriting Officer for its Structured Risk Solutions business. In this role, Halai will direct the global underwriting approach for the unit, enhancing AXA XL’s ability to deliver creative, non-traditional insurance solutions to corporate clients and captives. His leadership will be instrumental in continuing to evolve our offering and helping our clients transfer increasingly complex risks.” AXA XL’s Structured Risk Solutions team specialises in non-traditional insurance and reinsurance offerings for corporate clients and captives.

item.title: DOXA accelerates international expansion with UK MGU Eaton Gate acquisition
item.description: Eaton Gate is DOXA’s first acquisition in the UK and the firm expects it to be instrumental in accelerating its international expansion and growth strategy across specialty insurance. DOXA, a US-based managing general underwriter (MGU) platform, has acquired the Eaton Gate Group (Eaton Gate) from European alternative credit specialist, NorthWall Capital. Over the course of the partnership, the facility supported Eaton Gate’s continued regional expansion and investment in its e-trade platform.

item.title: Gallagher Re sets $115bn–$125bn loss threshold for meaningful pricing shift
item.description: A new Gallagher Re report has suggested that a single event, or a series of large events, resulting in an insured loss of $115 billion to $125 billion above expected average annual catastrophe losses, would be required to meaningfully impact the trajectory of pricing in the property re/insurance industry. Gallagher Re continued, “The post-2008 SCS loss environment has been significantly influenced by claims inflation and labour market constraints. Gallagher Re said this environment has left insurers’ annual catastrophe budgets well supported, placing the industry in an even stronger position to absorb future major individual events, as well as the cumulative impact of more frequent mid-sized losses.

item.title: Tariff Refund System Launches as Thousands of Companies File Claims
item.description: government went live on Monday as thousands of companies rushed to file claims. The refund system set up to allow companies to recover illegally collected tariffs from the U.S. I’m very happy we got this process started early.” Companies contacted by Reuters in recent days expressed concerns about the durability of the new system, created by U.S.

item.title: Supreme Court Rebuffs Challenge to Class-Action Status of Bank Collusion Suit
item.description: Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear a bid by Bank of America and seven other major financial institutions to prevent American cities from banding together in a $12 billion class action accusing them of artificially inflating interest rates on a popular municipal bond. The Supreme Court’s action paves the way for the suit to proceed as a class action. The justices turned away an appeal by the banks brought after a lower court upheld a judge’s decision to certify the lawsuit brought by Baltimore, Philadelphia, San Diego and other cities as a class action.

item.title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, which recently has been embraced by combat veterans and conservative lawmakers despite having serious safety risks. “Today’s order will ensure that people suffering from debilitating symptoms might finally have a chance to reclaim their lives and lead a happier life,” Trump said as he signed an executive order on the drugs. “It absolutely changed my life for the better.” The Food and Drug Administration next week will issue national priority vouchers for three psychedelics, which the agency’s commissioner, Marty Makary, said will allow certain drugs to be approved quickly “if they are in line with our national priorities.” The vouchers can cut review times from several months to a period of weeks.

item.title: BBVA activa score de seguridad en la app para informar nivel de protección de sus clientes
item.description: Desde la semana pasada, los clientes de la app del banco tienen acceso a un score de seguridad, una función que evalúa la cantidad de filtros, pasos de verificación y hasta la robustez de las contraseñas fijadas con el propósito de identificar qué tan protegido está el usuario. En una escala de 0% a 100%, el banco ahora informará el nivel de seguridad que tiene el usuario de la app y priorizará qué aspectos débiles en protección hay que reforzar para evitar fraudes o ataques cibernéticos. BBVA Colombia activó una nueva estrategia para garantizar la seguridad de las operaciones digitales.

item.title: Outdated cat models can’t keep pace with soaring secondary peril’ threats: Swiss Re warns
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hong Kong marine war risk pool seeks local capacity retention to wean off overseas markets, secure hub status
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ICA upgrades 2025 extreme weather loss total to US$3.4bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Lilly Acquiring Kelonia In Largest Funded Biotech Startup Purchase In Years
item.description: Per Crunchbase data, the high end of the purchase price represents the largest acquisition of a venture-backed biotech company in years. Kelonia’s vision is ambitious enough, and early results encouraging enough, to warrant what ranks as the largest acquisition of a venture-backed, private biotech company in the past 10 years, per Crunchbase data. Eli Lilly announced Monday that it is acquiring Kelonia Therapeutics , a developer of gene therapies with a particular focus on cancer treatment, in a deal valued at up to $7 billion in cash.

item.title: California Commissioner Advances Proposal to Overhaul Intervenor Process
item.description: California Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and the California Department of Insurance are continuing to try and revamp the state’s intervenor process, in which groups or people are paid for intervening in rate hearings. Related: California Insurance Commissioner Unveils Intervenor Reform Regulations The reforms follow months of public engagement, including input from consumer advocates, insurers, and members of the public. The reforms a component of Lara’s Sustainable Insurance Strategy, an overhaul of California’s insurance regulations aimed at stabilizing the market and expanding coverage options.

item.title: US Weighs Tougher Auto Import Rules to Accelerate Reshoring
item.description: “At the negotiation tables, there is no discussion of increasing auto tariffs or increasing rules of origin for autos,” Luis Rosendo Gutiérrez, Mexico’s deputy economy minister for trade, said Wednesday on the sidelines of an event in Washington. The Trump administration is considering potential changes to North American trade rules that would raise tariff costs on U.S. Administration officials have floated the potential changes internally and with people close to the auto industry ahead of a planned review of the so-called USMCA trade agreement, the people said.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic at Standstill After Iran Abruptly Ends Reopening
item.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is at a virtual standstill on Monday after a brief and confused reopening over the weekend ended with the first US seizure of an Iranian vessel — underscoring just how difficult it will be to restore activity in the vital strait. Related: Secret Codes and Yuan Fees Get Ships Through Iran’s Hormuz Tollbooth “The continued volatility will deter most, if not all shipowners, to adopt a cautious ‘wait and see’ approach,” said Ivan Mathews, head of APAC analysis at Vortexa Ltd. Transits through the waterway have reduced to a trickle over seven weeks of war in the Persian Gulf, as Iran tightens control in retaliation for U.S.

item.title: FBI Director Patel Suing Atlantic in $250 Million Defamation Case
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic and staff writer Sarah Fitzpatrick over a story alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences, calling the reporting “malicious lies” built on anonymous sources with partisan motives. The suit, filed on behalf of Patel by the Binnall Law Group, alleges The Atlantic gave the FBI less than two hours to respond to 19 detailed allegations, ignored a pre-publication letter identifying specific falsehoods, buried a denial from the FBI’s assistant director calling the claims “completely false at a nearly 100% clip,” and later stealth-edited the headline to “The FBI Director Is MIA.” Patel is seeking $250 million in damages, plus disgorgement of revenue earned from the story. “We stand by our reporting on Kash Patel, and we will vigorously defend The Atlantic and our journalists against this meritless lawsuit,” said Anna Bross, a spokesperson for the publication.

item.title: NYC Mayor Eyes City-Run Insurance Program for Affordable Housing
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani is seeking to lower the cost of property and liability insurance for affordable and rent-stabilized housing through a new city-run insurance program. According to the city, the cost of insurance has hit affordable and rent-stabilized housing particularly hard. The city believes the new proposal will also drive down city spending per home in its affordable housing programs because every $100 increase in insurance costs requires $1,200 more in city capital in new transactions.

item.title: AI Use in Cybersecurity Could Show Holes in Short Term, Says Fitch
item.description: Fitch said Anthropic’s Mythos model has raised eyebrows in the financial and cybersecurity worlds. In the short to medium term, vulnerabilities will probably outnumber patches as the artificial intelligence tool works on cyber threat intelligence and incident response. Related: Anthropic Touts AI Cybersecurity Project With Big Tech Partners “AI is particularly disruptive to cyber risk because traditional vulnerability analysis was labor-intensive and offered limited financial upside for researchers, a gap AI now fills at scale and speed,” said Fitch in its brief on the cyber marketplace on Feb.

item.title: India expands list of Russian P&I insurers: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: New Zealand hit by storm every 8 days as wild weather fuels 256% jump in claims: IAG
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Insurance-linked securities sector can be a big beneficiary of the AI revolution: SIFMA
item.description: The catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities sector can be a significant beneficiary of the revolution in artificial intelligence (AI), with applications such as process improvement, enhanced underwriting and analytics, as well as powering the ILS industry to do more, speakers discussed at the SIFMA ILS conference in Miami last week. One of the panels at SIFMA’s annual insurance-linked securities conference last week spoke about the implications of artificial intelligence within ILS and how the industry is adopting it. Bobby Bray, Senior Vice President, Insurance-Linked Securities Origination and Structuring at Guy Carpenter’s specialist capital markets unit GC Securities, said he sees meaningful potential for the industry to benefit from AI technologies.

item.title: Bermuda’s SPI and collateralized insurer uptake maintains momentum in Q1: BMA
item.description: Coming off a remarkable 2025, which saw the registration of new collateralized insurer class of companies in Bermuda climb to its highest level ever, early indicators for Q1 2026 suggest a continuation of this growth trend with seven new special purpose insurers (SPIs) and two new collateralized insurers having registered in Bermuda during the opening quarter. At the same time, a total of 10 new collateralized insurers were also registered in Bermuda last year too. According to the Authority’s latest market data, seven new SPIs and two new collateralized insurers have registered in Bermuda in Q1 2026, reflecting continued demand for fully collateralized reinsurance and capital markets structures.

item.title: Diego Sánchez Canencia joins Howden Re Iberia as Head of Analytics
item.description: Global insurance broker Howden has announced the appointment of Diego Sánchez Canencia as Head of Analytics, Howden Re Iberia, a Madrid-based role. In his new position, Sánchez Canencia will lead analytics delivery for Howden Re Iberia, working closely with local broking teams and the wider Howden Re Analytics platform to support clients with advanced actuarial analysis, pricing insight and reinsurance optimisation. David Santos Manzanares, Managing Director and Head of Iberia, Howden Re, said: “Diego’s appointment reflects our commitment to building best-in-class analytical capabilities across Iberia.

item.title: Trade credit insurance ‘a vital enabler of global trade’ as claim values rise in 2025: LMA’s Powell
item.description: David Powell, Head of Technical Underwriting at the Lloyd’s Market Association, said, “Trade credit insurance remains a vital enabler of global trade. While the number of global trade credit insurance claims fell 26.5% in 2025, the aggregate value rose by nearly $38 million year-on-year to $438.5 million, underscoring the importance of robust underwriting and long-term capacity to support complex transactions, according to David Powell, Head of Technical Underwriting at the Lloyd’s Market Association. The Lloyd’s Market Association, International Underwriting Association, and London & International Insurance Brokers’ Association published the results of their annual survey of global trade credit insurance claims, conducted by A2Z Risk Services.

item.title: BMA reports sustained growth in alternative capital and ILS market
item.description: Growth in this segment also supports Bermuda’s wider alternative capital market, which includes traditional insurers, Special Purpose Insurers (SPIs), and collateralised insurance arrangements that enable the transfer of catastrophe and other insurance risks to international capital markets. “The BMA provides a stable, transparent and efficient regulatory environment that underpins the continued growth and resilience of the alternative capital and ILS market. The Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA), the organisation responsible for regulating Bermuda’s financial services sector, has released data for Q4 2025 showing continued expansion in the island’s alternative capital and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market.

item.title: Destructive Winds and Tornadoes Leave Trail of Damage Across Midwest
item.description: on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris. In Olmsted County, Minnesota, sheriff’s officials said tornadoes caused “multiple levels” of damage. A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the U.S.

item.title: Low-Producing Oil Wells Cause Headaches for Texans
item.description: There are thousands of oil and gas wells around Texas like these: low-producing wells leased by companies operating on a shoestring. “The wells on the lease are all producing,” said Railroad Commission spokesperson Bryce Dubee. Some Texas oil wells gush hundreds of barrels of oil a day.

item.title: People Moves: Swingle Collins & Associates Appoints Curtis as CEO
item.description: Swingle Collins & Associates announced the appointment of Dan Curtis as Chief Executive Officer, effective March 31, 2026. Curtis joined Swingle Collins one year ago as President, quickly establishing himself as a transformative leader committed to the agency’s core values of passion, expertise, advocacy, collaboration, and empathy. As CEO, Curtis will guide Swingle Collins through phases of and innovation and expansion as the agency explores new technologies, service offerings and partners across complex middle market commercial, high net worth personal, and employee benefits insurance.

item.title: Ageas expands UK personal lines reach through Sky Protect partnership
item.description: Ageas UK has partnered with Sky Protect to join its growing home insurance panel, marking a strategic step to broaden distribution and scale in the UK personal lines market. By leveraging Sky’s extensive customer reach, Ageas can expand its footprint without the need to build new direct channels. For Sky Protect, the addition of Ageas strengthens its multi-insurer panel, enabling more competitive pricing and greater flexibility for customers seeking tailored home insurance solutions.

item.title: ManyPets and Vet-AI redefine pet insurance experience with integrated digital care
item.description: The post ManyPets and Vet-AI redefine pet insurance experience with integrated digital care appeared first on Insurtech Insights . ManyPets and Vet-AI have joined forces to deliver a more connected approach to pet health, combining insurance and veterinary expertise within a single digital journey. It also demonstrates how AI and digital health tools can enhance customer experience while improving outcomes for both pets and providers.

item.title: AXA XL names global CUO for structured risk solutions
item.description: AXA XL appointed Jiten Halai as global chief underwriting officer for structured risk solutions, reinforcing its push into alternative risk transfer. In the role, Halai will lead global underwriting strategy for structured risk solutions, working across regions to expand AXA XL’s capabilities in non traditional insurance for corporates and captives. AXA XL’s structured risk solutions unit focuses on customized offerings, including performance insurance for emerging technologies and complex risk transfer programs.

item.title: Achmea appoints Rogier Peters as CRO
item.description: Achmea has appointed Rogier Peters as a member of its Executive Board and Chief Risk Officer, effective October 1, 2026. Peters joins from MSIG Europe , where he has served as CRO since 2020, focusing on property, casualty, and marine lines. Following the appointment, the Executive Board will consist of Chair Bianca Tetteroo, CFO Michel Lamie, Peters as CRO, along with Daphne de Kluis, Robert Otto, and Lidwien Suur.

item.title: Anthony Izzo joins AXIS as Chief Commercial Officer
item.description: AXIS Capital announced the appointment of Anthony Izzo as Chief Commercial Officer. Izzo will lead the company’s global commercial strategy, working closely with its underwriting and regional leadership to enhance how AXIS engages with brokers and partners to accelerate profitable growth and advance its multi-variant distribution strategy while unlocking new business opportunities. Izzo joins AXIS with 35 years of industry experience.

item.title: Chubb Malaysia hires Allanz Singapore’s Ariel Llugdar as head of motor claims
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: US$76bn APAC nat cat losses in 2025 as protection gap widens: Aon

item.title: 7.5-magnitude earthquake off northeast Japan triggers tsunami alerts, evacuations
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Join the Financial Services Consumer Panel – vacancies now open
item.description: We are recruiting 2 new members to the Financial Services Consumer Panel , an independent statutory panel that represents the interests of consumers of financial services to the FCA. Panel members provide constructive challenge and expert advice to help ensure the consumer perspective is fully embedded in the FCA’s policy development and implementation. Help shape financial regulation from the perspective of consumers.

item.title: Payments Modernization Is Insurance’s Next Big Margin Engine
item.description: Margin compression is forcing insurers to rethink payments as a cost center. One of the biggest and most overlooked drivers of margin compression sits inside the payments function. According to One Inc CEO Ian Drysdale , payments modernization is no longer a technology upgrade.

item.title: Cybersecurity Funding Holds Up At Robust Levels
item.description: Cybersecurity tends to be one of the more resilient sectors for startup funding, as customers know it’s cheaper in the long run to pay for it than go without. This past quarter, funding to security- and privacy-focused startups dipped slightly on a sequential basis, but remained well above year-ago levels. For a broader view of large funding recipients, below we put together a list of 10 of the largest Q1 cybersecurity rounds.

item.title: ‘Very likely’ cat bonds will be used to source risk capital for data centre build out: John Seo
item.description: As the data centre build-out continues to expand as an emerging field across the industry, it appears very likely that insurers, reinsurers and various organisations may turn towards catastrophe bonds to source risk capital for these facilities, according to John Seo of Fermat Capital Management LLC. During a recent webinar hosted by Fermat Capital Management, Co-Founder and Managing Director John Seo shared insights into why these high-value assets are increasingly seeking protection from the ILS market. So now what we’re seeing is a huge shift in economic activity to the centre of the US, and a lot of that is actually being led by AI data centres.” He continued: “Turns out you can’t take an AI data centre and put it on the coastlines, because you’ll crash the grid.

item.title: Magnitude 7.5 earthquake hits off Japan coast, tsunami warnings in effect
item.description: A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck off the northeastern Japanese coast along the Sanriku region of Hokkaido and Iwate prefecture at around 4.50pm local time Monday (8.50am UK), with small tsunami waves already reported and a warning of the potential for higher waves imminently. Media reports suggest current warnings for tsunami waves are ongoing, with waves of up to 3 metres possible and Iwate prefecture seen as the region facing the greatest threat, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. Tsunami warnings are in effect for Hokkaido and Aomori for waves of up to 3 metres, as well as advisories for Miyagi and Fukushima prefectures for waves of up to 1 meter.

item.title: Lloyd’s market remains a compelling opportunity for investors: LMA / ICMR
item.description: At the same time, the report also indicated that, with individual syndicates delivering strong results throughout 2025, “Lloyd’s remains well positioned as a compelling proposition to investors.” As previously noted, the Lloyd’s market has also seen an increasing number of new entrants and a growth in risk capital deployed through the market’s ILS structure London Bridge 2 PCC. Lloyd’s of London continued to demonstrate its attractiveness to investors throughout 2025, with individual syndicates delivering strong results, while the market has also seen an increasing number of new entrants and a growth in risk capital deployed through the market’s insurance-linked securities (ILS) structure London Bridge 2 PCC. The Lloyd’s Market Association (LMA) and Insurance Capital Markets Research (ICMR) recently published its Lloyd’s 2026 Insights Report, which provides a detailed analysis of Lloyd’s and syndicates’ 2025 year-end results.
